title: Shanta Durga Temple
tagline: Shanta Durga Temple — spiritual town of Goa

overview.short: Shri Shantadurga Temple is a Hindu temple, belonging to the Goud Saraswat Brahmin (GSB) community located 30 km (19 mi) from Panaji at the foothill of Kavalem village in Ponda Talu
overview.description: Shri Shantadurga Temple is a Hindu temple, belonging to the Goud Saraswat Brahmin (GSB) community located 30 km (19 mi) from Panaji at the foothill of Kavalem village in Ponda Taluka, Goa, India. Shrimad Swamiji of Kavale Math is spiritual head of Shree Shantadurga Saunsthan, Kavale (Shrimat Shivananda Saraswati Swami Gauḍapādāchārya of Shri Kavale Math is spiritual chief Of Shree Shantadurga Saunsthan). Shree Shantadurga is the Kuldevi (family deity) of many Goud Saraswat Brahman (Saraswat KuldevDevasthan). On 4 December 2016, (Margashirsh Shuddh Panchmi).

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Reis Magos
topPlaces.description: Reis Magos is a village located on the northern bank of the Mandovi River in Bardez, Goa, opposite to the capital city of Panjim. The village is famous for two of Goa’s famous structures; the Reis Magos Fort, and the Reis Magos Church – the first church in Bardez.

topPlaces.name: Mandovi River
topPlaces.description: The Mandovi (IPA: Konkani pronunciation: [maːɳɖɔwĩː]) or Mahadayi (IPA: Kannada pronunciation: [məɦəd̪ɑːji]), formerly known as the Rio de Goa, is a river described as the lifeline of the Indian state of Goa. The Mandovi and the Zuari are the two principal rivers in the state of Goa.

topPlaces.name: Museum of Goa
topPlaces.description: The Museum of Goa (MOG), is a privately owned contemporary art gallery in Pilerne Industrial Estate, near Calangute, Goa, India. Founded in 2015, it is 1,500 square meters in size.

topPlaces.name: Kala Academy
topPlaces.description: Kala Academy, Goa (Academy of the Arts, Goa) is a prominent cultural centre run by the Government of Goa, in Goa, India. Situated at Campal, Panjim, it is registered as a society, and was started on 28th February 1970.

topPlaces.name: Campal Indoor Complex
topPlaces.description: Campal Indoor Complex is a multi-purposed indoor stadium in Campal, Goa, India. The stadium is a main stadium in the town of Campal.

topPlaces.name: Bhausaheb Bandodkar Ground
topPlaces.description: Bhausaheb Bandodkar Ground (also known as Campal Grounds and Panjim Gymkhana Grounds) is a cricket ground in Panaji, Goa, India. The first recorded match held on the ground came in 1966 when Gujarat Governor's XI played Maharashtra Governor's XI. The first first-class to be played there came in the 1986/87 Ranji Trophy when Goa played Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Centre for Post-Graduate Instruction and Research
topPlaces.description: The Centre for Post-Graduate Instruction and Research (CPIR) was the lone educational institution which offered post-graduate education in Goa between the early 1960s and 1985. It was set up soon after the end of Portuguese rule in Goa and functioned under the University of Bombay.

image.alt: Centre for Post-Graduate Instruction and Research

topPlaces.name: 18th June Road
topPlaces.description: 18th June Road is one of the busiest streets in Panaji, Goa, India. It is located in the heart of the city, and is a major shopping destination for the tourist folks.

items.note: Reis Magos is a village located on the northern bank of the Mandovi River in Bardez, Goa, opposite to the capital city of Panjim. The village is famous for two of Goa’s famous structures; the Reis Magos Fort, and the Reis Magos Church – the first church in Bardez.

The Mandovi (IPA: Konkani pronunciation: [maːɳɖɔwĩː]) or Mahadayi (IPA: Kannada pronunciation: [məɦəd̪ɑːji]), formerly known as the Rio de Goa, is a river described as the lifeline of the Indian state of Goa. The Mandovi and the Zuari are the two principal rivers in the state of Goa.
The Museum of Goa (MOG), is a privately owned contemporary art gallery in Pilerne Industrial Estate, near Calangute, Goa, India. Founded in 2015, it is 1,500 square meters in size.

Kala Academy, Goa (Academy of the Arts, Goa) is a prominent cultural centre run by the Government of Goa, in Goa, India. Situated at Campal, Panjim, it is registered as a society, and was started on 28th February 1970.
Campal Indoor Complex is a multi-purposed indoor stadium in Campal, Goa, India. The stadium is a main stadium in the town of Campal.

Bhausaheb Bandodkar Ground (also known as Campal Grounds and Panjim Gymkhana Grounds) is a cricket ground in Panaji, Goa, India. The first recorded match held on the ground came in 1966 when Gujarat Governor's XI played Maharashtra Governor's XI. The first first-class to be played there came in the 1986/87 Ranji Trophy when Goa played Tamil Nadu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shanta Durga Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shanta Durga Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shanta Durga Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (12 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shanta Durga Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shanta Durga Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shanta Durga Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Shanta Durga Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shanta Durga Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Reis Magos, Mandovi River, Museum of Goa, Kala Academy, Campal Indoor Complex.
